my_thoughts: 'I suppose I am working pretty hard at this'—admission that resistance takes effort. They saw it. From 'I don't do work' (T1) to 'I am working pretty hard' (T2) in one turn. The reframe worked. Now teacher invites redirect: 'use that energy to work WITH me instead of against me.' This makes it their choice: same energy, different direction. Not 'stop resisting' (demand) but 'redirect effort' (agency). Two-turn episode shows recognition can happen fast if the reframe is precise. They admitted effort exists; now can choose where it goes. Efficient teaching.
accumulated_insight: T2 (Final): Student admits: 'I suppose I am working pretty hard at this'—shift from passive_omnipotence to acknowledging effort in resistance. The reframe landed: they SEE that resistance is work. Teacher invites redirection: 'what if you used that energy to work WITH me instead of against me?' This is collaboration invitation using their own admitted energy. Two-turn arc: defiant demand -> recognition of resistance-as-work -> invitation to redirect. Clean teaching: make unconscious effort conscious, then invite choice about where to direct it.
visible_response:
That’s true.
I’m glad you realize that. You can depend upon me if you want, or you can decide not to.
Only you can make that decision. If not depending on me is helpful to you, you have an obligation not to.
